blocks.body: Factoring is the reverse of distributing. Instead of multiplying out, you find what was multiplied together to get the expression.
The largest factor shared by all terms. To factor using GCF: find the GCF of all coefficients and the lowest power of any common variable. Factor it out.
Steps: (1) Find GCF of all terms. (2) Divide each term by the GCF. (3) Write as GCF x (remaining polynomial). Always check by distributing back.
Always look for a GCF first, before trying any other factoring method. It simplifies the remaining expression and makes subsequent steps easier.
